channel.title: Conditions We Treat on Zhenchi Kalpa Acupuncture
channel.description: Recent content in Conditions We Treat on Zhenchi Kalpa Acupuncture

item.title: Diabetic Foot Ulcers: Understanding Symptoms, Causes and Supportive Care
item.description:
Diabetic Foot Ulcers

Diabetic foot ulcers are a common complication of diabetes that can affect mobility, comfort, and overall quality of life. Early recognition and appropriate management are important to reduce the risk of infection and other serious complications.

What Are Diabetic Foot Ulcers?

A diabetic foot ulcer is an open sore or wound that develops on the foot in individuals with diabetes. These ulcers often occur due to a combination of nerve damage (diabetic neuropathy), reduced circulation, pressure on the feet, and delayed wound healing.

item.title: Fever: Understanding Symptoms, Causes and Supportive Care
item.description:
Fever

Fever is one of the most common reasons people seek healthcare. While it can be uncomfortable, fever is often regarded as part of the body’s response to illness.

What Is Fever?

Fever refers to an elevation in body temperature above the normal range. It may occur in response to infections, inflammatory conditions, or other physiological processes.

Common Symptoms

  • Increased body temperature
  • Chills
  • Fatigue
  • Sweating
  • Body aches
  • Reduced appetite

Common Causes

  • Viral infections
  • Bacterial infections
  • Inflammatory conditions
  • Reactions to medications
  • Other medical conditions

Individualized Assessment

Every person experiences illness differently. At Zhenchi Kalpa Acupuncture Clinic, each patient is assessed individually, taking into account symptoms, overall health, and personal circumstances.
